title: 10 best herbal teas for numbness in hands
intro_desc: Herbal teas for numbness in hands are natural remedies that help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The best herbal teas for numbness in hands include Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Capsicum annuum, Valeriana officinalis, Cinnamomum verum, Passiflora incarnata, Avena sativa, Piper nigrum, Curcuma longa, and Corydalis yanhusuo. If left untreated, numbness in hands can lead to reduced dexterity, impaired fine motor skills, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life, work, and overall quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a natural remedy that can help with numbness in hands. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The tea is made from these leaves and contains powerful antioxidants that can improve blood flow to the hands. When blood flow is good, it helps to deliver oxygen and nutrients to the nerves in the hands, which can help to reduce numbness. Ginkgo biloba tea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and pain in the hands. This can be especially helpful for people who experience numbness due to poor circulation or injury to the nerves in their hands. Drinking Ginkgo biloba tea regularly may also help to improve nerve function and reduce the risk of numbness and tingling in the hands.

Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, has been used for centuries to help people with numbness in their hands. The main reason for this is that ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the nerves and improve blood flow to the hands. When the blood flow is good, it helps to carry oxygen and nutrients to the nerves, which can help reduce numbness and tingling sensations. Ginger tea also has a compound called gingerol, which has antioxidant properties that help protect the nerves from damage. By drinking ginger tea, people with numbness in their hands may experience relief from their symptoms and feel more comfortable in their daily activities. Additionally, ginger tea has been shown to help reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness, which can help improve the quality of life for people who suffer from this condition.

Cinnamomum verum tea, also known as Ceylon cinnamon tea, has been used to help relieve numbness in hands due to its anti-inflammatory properties. When numbness occurs, it can be caused by reduced blood flow to the hands, which Ceylon cinnamon tea helps to improve by widening blood vessels. This increased blood flow brings more oxygen and nutrients to the hands, helping to reduce numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon tea contains antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the hands. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with numbness. Furthermore, Ceylon cinnamon tea has been shown to improve circulation and nerve function, which can help to alleviate numbness and other symptoms. Regular consumption of Ceylon cinnamon tea may help to reduce numbness in hands and promote overall health and well-being.

Valeriana officinalis tea has been used for centuries to help with numbness in hands. This herbal tea is made from the root of the valerian plant, which is rich in a compound called valerenic acid. This acid has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the hands, which can cause numbness. The tea also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can contribute to numbness. When we are under stress, our nervous system can become overactive, causing numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Valeriana officinalis tea can help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, which can help to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, the tea has antioxidant properties that can help to protect the nerves from damage and promote healthy nerve function. Regular consumption of Valeriana officinalis tea may help to reduce numbness in the hands and promote overall hand health.

Passiflora incarnata tea is used to help people with numbness in their hands. This is because the tea contains a compound called harmine, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When inflammation is present in the body, it can cause numbness and pain in the hands. The harmine in Passiflora incarnata tea helps to reduce inflammation, which in turn can help to reduce numbness. Additionally, the tea contains other compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids, which have antioxidant properties. These antioxidants help to protect the nerves from damage and promote healthy nerve function. This can help to improve sensation in the hands and reduce numbness. Passiflora incarnata tea is often used in conjunction with other natural remedies to help alleviate numbness and other nerve-related symptoms. Some people also use it to help with stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to numbness in the hands.

Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is a herbal remedy that may help with numbness in hands. This is because rosemary contains a compound called carnosic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When inflammation occurs in the nerves, it can cause numbness or tingling sensations in the hands. By reducing inflammation, rosemary tea may help to alleviate these symptoms. Additionally, rosemary tea contains antioxidants that help protect the nerves from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to promote healthy nerve function and reduce numbness in the hands. Furthermore, rosemary tea has been traditionally used to improve circulation, which is essential for delivering oxygen and nutrients to the nerves and may also help to reduce numbness.

Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is a popular herbal remedy for numbness in hands. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pressure on nerves, which can cause numbness. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can also improve blood flow to the hands, which can help restore feeling and sensation. Additionally, the tea's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to numbness and tingling in the hands. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea regularly may also help to relax muscles and tendons, which can become tight and compress nerves, leading to numbness. Furthermore, the tea's antioxidant properties can help protect nerves from damage and promote healthy nerve function.

Achillea millefolium tea, also known as yarrow tea, is a natural remedy that may help relieve numbness in hands. The tea contains compounds that help improve blood circulation, which is essential for delivering oxygen and nutrients to the hands. When blood circulation is poor, numbness and tingling sensations can occur. Drinking yarrow tea may help dilate blood vessels and increase blood flow to the hands, reducing numbness and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow tea may also help reduce swelling and pain in the hands, making it easier to move and use them comfortably. Additionally, yarrow tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the hands from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to numbness and other health problems.

Capsicum annuum tea is made from the pepper plant, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the hands. The capsaicin in the tea, a natural compound found in chili peppers, blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This means that when you take Capsicum annuum tea, you may feel a reduction in numbness and tingling sensations in your hands. The tea also increases blood flow to the affected area, which helps to reduce swelling and promote healing. Additionally, Capsicum annuum tea has antioxidant properties that protect the nerves and surrounding tissues from damage. By taking the tea regularly, some people may experience relief from numbness and discomfort in their hands, allowing them to perform daily tasks with more ease and comfort.

Eucalyptus globulus tea is a natural remedy that may help with numbness in hands. The tea is made from the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. When we experience numbness in our hands, it's often due to poor blood circulation or nerve damage. The anti-inflammatory properties in eucalyptus globulus tea may help reduce inflammation and promote better blood flow to the affected areas, which can help ease numbness and tingling sensations. Additionally, the tea's decongestant properties can help improve circulation and reduce swelling in the hands, further contributing to relief from numbness. Some people also find that eucalyptus globulus tea helps relax muscles and reduce stress, which can contribute to numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Drinking the tea regularly may help alleviate these symptoms and provide relief from numbness in the hands.
